title: Smoky Spanish Chicken Roast
description: A fragrant, smoky roast chicken with citrus zest and fragrant makrut lime leaves, perfect for a romantic dinner that is fodmap-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius.
Pat the chicken dry with paper towels and season the cavity with smoked sea salt and black pepper.
In a small bowl, combine smoked paprika, orange zest, dried rosemary, dried thyme, and a splash of orange juice to form a thick paste.
Rub the paste all over the chicken, making sure to get it under the skin and inside the cavity.
Tuck the makrut lime leaves in the cavity and around the chicken.
Drizzle the olive oil over the chicken and place it breast-side up on a rimmed baking sheet.
Roast the chicken for 30 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 180 degrees Celsius and continue roasting for another 60 minutes.
Baste the chicken every 20 minutes with pan juices to keep the skin crisp.
Check that the internal temperature in the thickest part reaches 75 degrees Celsius before removing from the oven.
Let the chicken rest for 10 minutes before carving and serving with a side of roasted vegetables if desired.
